title: Waste Management: Ministry of the Environment Welcomes Tunisian Delegation to Promote Biomethane Production from Waste
content:
From February 10 to 13, the Italian Ministry of the Environment and Energy Security will host an institutional Tunisian delegation comprising representatives from the Ministries of Energy and Economy, national agencies responsible for energy, waste management, and sanitation, as well as the local office of the United Nations Development Programme (UNDP).
The Tunisian delegation, keen to explore Italian regulatory models following the introduction of the biogas incentive tariff (Ministerial Decree of August 29, 2025), is visiting Italy to learn from best practices and to accelerate the transition toward sustainable waste management.
The meeting will also provide Tunisia with the opportunity to present its strategy and the pilot project for biomethane production from waste launched on the island of Djerba, implemented under the â€œPiattaforma di supporto agli investimenti e assistenza tecnicaâ€ (Investment Support and Technical Assistance Platform programme ) launched in 2024 by the Italian Ministry of the Environment and Energy Security (MASE) in partnership with UNDP. Following the first positive results, the Tunisian authorities plan to scale up the initiative nationwide, with feasibility studies scheduled for three to four additional sites.

The mission, taking place between Rome and Milan, will include roundtable discussions, institutional meetings with leading engineering firms and key stakeholders, as well as technical and operational working sessions aimed at fostering the development of innovative and sustainable solutions. The programme also features technical site visits to centres of excellence such as ACEA, A2A, and the Municipality of Milan, a recognized European best practice in integrated waste management. Italian companies will have the opportunity to share best practices, identify synergies, and contribute to the creation of a virtuous cycle of innovation and international partnerships.
The opening is scheduled for Tuesday, February 10, at the UNDP Centre in Rome, with a high-level roundtable promoted by the MASE, Directorate for European and International Affairs. The event aims to strengthen cooperation between Italy and Tunisia in advancing energy recovery from waste. The roundtable will bring together senior representatives of Italian and Tunisian institutions, alongside leading figures from the private sector and key agencies active in waste management and energy efficiency.
â€œThe visit of the Tunisian delegation fully falls within the framework of the Mattei Plan for Africa,â€ underlined Alessandro Guerri, Director. â€œIt is a strategy promoted by the Government of Italy to strengthen partnerships with African countries in the fields of sustainable development and resilient infrastructure, while promoting circular economy solutions and technology transfer across the Mediterranean region.â€
author: Analysts Account
source: Italian Government, Ministry of the Environment
